macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: IMF Releases World Economic Outlook Reporting Slowing Global Growth and Renewed Inflationary Pressures
items.summary: The IMF's latest World Economic Outlook highlights slowing global growth amid renewed inflationary pressures, urging agile policies to manage trade-offs like ramping up defense spending. This was presented during the IMF and World Bank Spring Meetings.

items.title: G24 Leaders Hold Press Conference on Global Economic and Geopolitical Issues
items.summary: G24 leaders convened a major press conference in Washington DC, addressing pressing global economic and geopolitical challenges amid ongoing international tensions.

items.title: Fed Chair Powell Scheduled to Speak Amid Key US Economic Data Releases
items.summary: Fed Chair Powell is set to deliver a speech at 02:30 PM, coinciding with releases like Dallas Fed Manufacturing Index (-0.2 vs. 0.2 expected) and other indicators including Chicago PMI and JOLTs Job Openings.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Surges Past $75,000 for First Time in Over a Month
items.summary: Bitcoin reached a one-month high above $75,000, up 6% in the past 24 hours to around $75,300, testing a key 'structural breakout' level amid reduced geopolitical tensions.

items.title: Bitcoin Short Squeeze Triggers $541 Million in Crypto Liquidations
items.summary: Bitcoin's 5.7% gain to $74,679 over the past 24 hours sparked $540 million in derivatives market liquidations, primarily shorts.

items.title: Ethereum Outperforms with 9% Rally as Bitcoin Tops $75,000
items.summary: Ether led the crypto rally with a 9% increase to $2,378 over the past 24 hours, surpassing Bitcoin's 5% gain to $74,586-$75,420, fueled by optimism over US-Iran peace talks.

items.title: Bitcoin and Ethereum Rally on Trump-Iran Peace Talk Signals
items.summary: Bitcoin rose 5% to near $75,000 and Ethereum climbed amid President Trump's openness to renewed talks with Iran, easing Middle East tensions and boosting market sentiment.

items.title: Crypto ETFs See Major Inflows Amid Market Recovery
items.summary: Bitcoin ETFs recorded $833 million in inflows last week, while Ethereum ETFs saw $187 million, reflecting optimism as the total crypto market cap stands at $2.6 trillion.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Anthropic Withholds Release of Powerful New AI Model Claude Mythos Due to Cybersecurity Risks
items.summary: Anthropic has launched Claude Mythos preview, its most powerful model to date, but is not releasing it publicly after it identified thousands of vulnerabilities across major OS and browsers. The company initiated Project Glasswing, backed by $100M in credits and $4M in donations, to patch these before hackers exploit them.[1][2]

items.title: AI's Environmental Impact on Sustainable Energy Debated Amid Efficiency Gains
items.summary: Concerns rise over AI data centers straining renewable energy, but experts note AI algorithms are becoming far more energy-efficient, with Google's Gemini emissions dropping 44x in one year, potentially offsetting demand increases.[3]

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Grand Theft Auto 6 Delayed to May 2026
items.summary: Rockstar Games has delayed Grand Theft Auto 6 to May 26, 2026, delivering the anticipated but dreaded news to fans.

items.title: Pragmata Launches with High Metacritic Score
items.summary: Pragmata debuts as one of 2026's top games with an 86 Metacritic score from 87 reviews, praised for its hacking combat.

items.title: Windrose Enters Early Access on Steam
items.summary: Co-op pirate survival game Windrose launches in early access after strong demo performance, offering large ocean world exploration with plans for 1-3 years of development.

items.title: Amazon Luna Winding Down, Players Lose Purchased Games
items.summary: Amazon is shutting down Luna, leaving players unable to access purchased titles even at locations like Denver airport.

items.title: Oblivion Remake Officially Returns May 15
items.summary: The Elder Scrolls IV: Oblivion Remake is set for release on May 15, with Bethesda confirming its return via teaser.

items.title: First Three Resident Evil Games Now on Steam
items.summary: The original Resident Evil titles and Breath of Fire IV are now available on PC via Steam.

items.title: Thick As Thieves Shifts to Single-Player and Co-Op
items.summary: Immersive sim heist game Thick As Thieves abandons PvPvE mode to focus solely on single-player and co-op.

items.title: Roblox Introduces New Pyramid Scheme-Like Subscription
items.summary: Roblox unveils a controversial new monetization model described as pyramid-shaped.

items.title: Nintendo Sues Alleged Switch 2 Leaker
items.summary: Nintendo takes legal action against accessory maker Genki for allegedly misleading consumers and leaking Switch 2 info.

items.title: Pokemon Champions Launches to Chaos
items.summary: New Pokemon Champions game faces rough launch with upset trainers amid ongoing issues.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.
